data.body: The /dso:onboarding skill asks the user questions whose answers are already available in project config files. For example, it asks 'I detected Husky pre-commit hooks and lint-staged. What does the pre-commit hook run?' — but the answer is right there in .husky/pre-commit and the lint-staged config in package.json. The skill should read these files and present the detected configuration for confirmation rather than asking open-ended questions. Other likely examples: test frameworks (detectable from package.json/pyproject.toml), build commands (detectable from scripts/Makefile), linter configs (detectable from config files). The fix should shift the Socratic dialogue toward confirmation of auto-detected facts rather than asking the user to describe what the tool can already read.
